content:
What are the Top Grants for Biotech & Life Sciences?

{/* IRAP Life Sciences */}
IRAP Life Sciences & Biotechnology Innovation
Up to $500K
Biotech R&D
Non-Repayable

IRAP funding specifically for life sciences SMEs developing innovative biotechnology solutions, medical devices, diagnostics, therapeutics, and health technology applications.

Eligible Life Sciences Projects:
  • • Drug discovery and development
  • • Medical device prototypes
  • • Diagnostic technology development
  • • Biomanufacturing process innovation
Funding Support:
  • • 60-80% of eligible R&D costs
  • • Industrial Technology Advisor support
  • • Access to research networks
  • • Commercialization guidance
{/* Clinical Trials Funding */}
Clinical Trials & Health Research Funding
Up to $10M
Clinical Studies
Phase I-IV

Support for clinical trials through CIHR, provincial health research programs, and specialized funding for drug trials, medical device testing, and health technology validation studies.

Clinical Trial Types:
  • • Phase I safety studies
  • • Phase II efficacy trials
  • • Phase III confirmatory trials
  • • Phase IV post-market surveillance
Funding Coverage:
  • • Patient recruitment costs
  • • Clinical site management
  • • Data collection and analysis
  • • Regulatory submission support
{/* Medical Device Pathways */}
Medical Device Development & Regulatory Pathways
Up to $3M
Device Innovation
Class I-IV

Specialized funding for medical device companies through design, prototyping, testing, regulatory approval (Health Canada), and commercialization stages.

Medical Device Categories:
  • • Diagnostic imaging devices
  • • Surgical instruments and implants
  • • Monitoring and wearable devices
  • • Therapeutic medical equipment
Regulatory Support:
  • • Health Canada MDEL submission
  • • FDA regulatory pathway guidance
  • • CE marking for EU markets
  • • Quality management system (ISO 13485)
{/* Biomanufacturing Support */}
Biomanufacturing & Scale-Up Funding
Up to $20M
Manufacturing
GMP Facilities

Strategic support for biomanufacturing facilities, scale-up operations, GMP manufacturing, and pharmaceutical production capacity through federal and provincial programs.

Biomanufacturing Focus:
  • • Biologics production facilities
  • • Cell and gene therapy manufacturing
  • • Vaccine production capacity
  • • API (Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient)
Scale-Up Support:
  • • GMP facility construction/upgrade
  • • Manufacturing equipment acquisition
  • • Process validation and optimization
  • • Quality assurance systems
shortAnswer: Yes — Complete guide to Canadian life sciences innovation grants. Access $720M+ funding through biomanufacturing programs, medical device pathways, clinical trials support, and 18+ biotechnology funding. Funding available: up to $720M+.
shortAnswerQuestion: What innovation and technology grants are available in 2026?

faq.question: What is the application deadline for Canada Life Sciences Grants  | + Biotech & Medical Device Funding | Clinical Trials Support in 2026?
faq.answer: Deadlines typically vary by specific program tier. However, for 2026, many initiatives operate on a rolling intake basis until funds are depleted. Check the official portal for specific cutoffs.

faq.question: How much funding can I get through Canada Life Sciences Grants  | + Biotech & Medical Device Funding | Clinical Trials Support?
faq.answer: Funding amounts range depending on business size and scope. Top awards can reach $720M.

faq.question: Do I have to give up equity for this program?
faq.answer: No. Government grants and subsidies are non-dilutive, meaning you retain 100% ownership of your company.

expertTip.title: Biomanufacturing Focus
expertTip.content: The federal government is prioritizing domestic biomanufacturing capacity. Projects that build Canadian production capabilities for vaccines and therapeutics are top priority.
